response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial and distributed systems experience — the GISCollective platform, Green Map GIS work, and high-throughput microservices at Sociomantic demonstrate relevant adjacent skills. However, the role requires C++ or Rust as a primary language, and his C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a bachelor's thesis project. His current and recent roles are almost entirely TypeScript/Node.js. There is no evidence of routing pipeline work, tile-based data formats, navigation algorithms, or the kind of low-latency systems-level C++/Rust development the role demands at senior depth.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map GIS app, FOSS4G speaker) shows domain familiarity
Proven distributed systems work at scale — real-time microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Broad technical range including Linux, low-latency async I/O (DLang), and embedded/hardware work demonstrates systems thinking
concerns: C++ experience is shallow and over a decade old; no Rust experience mentioned anywhere in the resume
No routing or navigation-specific work — no tile formats, routing graphs, traffic signal integration, or pipeline latency optimization
Current trajectory is firmly in TypeScript/Node.js and AI tooling, moving further from the systems-level C++ domain this role requires
